questions.stem: What is the output of the code segment to the right?
questions.code:
int a = 10, b = 4, c = 4;
out.print(a << 2 + b >> 1 + ++c);

questions.stem: What is the output of the code segment to the right?
What is the order of precedence for the operators to the right?
What is output by the code to the right?
questions.explanation: D Multiplication first: 4 ∗2 = 8, so 3 + 8 = 11. 11 > 10 is true, so ternary gives 1.

questions.stem: What is the output of the code segment to the right?
questions.code:
int a = 15, b = 12, c = 9 ;
out.print(c ^ b | a - c & b ^ a);

questions.stem: What is the output of the code segment to the right?
questions.explanation: A int H = 10 << 2; H = 40 int J = H >> 4; J = 2 int K = H >> J; K = 40 >> 2 K=10

questions.stem: What is output by the code to the right?
questions.explanation: B Order of precedence is ~, then >>, then &. ~3 = -4 4 >> 2 = 1 -4 & 1 = 0

questions.stem: What is output by the code to the right?
questions.explanation: C x + y << z is (x+y)<<z = 3<<3 = 24, x | y & z is x|(y&z) = 1|(2&3)=3, and the condition is true (due to || / && precedence and short-circuiting) so it adds 4, giving 24 ^ (3+4) = 24 ^ 7 = 31.

questions.stem: What is the output of the code segment to the right?
questions.explanation: D The first print statement prints 22, then adds one to W. The next, adds one more, then prints 24. The next, uses integer division twice, printing the value 6. W does not change. It is still 24. The last, doubles the value 24 twice, printing 96.

questions.stem: What is the output of the code segment to the right?
What is the order of precedence for the operators to the right?
What is output by the code to the right?
questions.explanation: C ++b increments b to 5 and uses 5, c-- uses 2 then decrements c to 1, b % c uses current values 5 % 1 = 0, then 5 * 2 = 10, and finally 3 + 10 - 0 = 13.

questions.explanation: A ++a pre-increments a to 6 and b-- post-decrements b (using 3 in the expression, then dropping to 2), so d = 6 - 3 = 3, and since a > b (6 > 2) is true the ternary picks a + c = 8, giving 3|8. UIL Computer Science Written Test – 2025-2026 State
